author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For unto Angels He hath not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man that Thou art mindful of him, or the son of man that Thou visitest him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou madest him a little lower than the Angels.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I could have wished to know for certain whether any hear with fitting earnestness the things that are said, whether we are not casting the seeds by the wayside: for in that case I should have made my instructions with more cheerfulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For we shall speak, though no one hear, for the fear which is laid on us by our Saviour.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, saith He, testify to this people; even if they hear not, thou shalt thyself be guiltless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (See Ezek. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If however I had been persuaded of your earnestness, I should have spoken not for fear only, but should have done it with pleasure also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For now indeed, even if no man hear, even if my work, so long as I fulfill my own part, brings no danger, still the labor is not altogether pleasant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what profit is it, when though I be not blamed, yet no one is benefited?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if any would give heed we shall receive advantage not so much from avoiding punishment ourselves as from your progress.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How then shall I know this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Having taken notice of some of you, who are not very attentive, I shall question them privately, when I meet them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And if I find that they retain any of the things that have been spoken (I say not all, for this would not be very easy for you), but even if [they retain] a few things out of many, it is plain I should have no further doubts about the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And indeed we ought, without giving notice beforehand, to have attacked you when off your guard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However it will suffice, if even in this way I should be able to attain my purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nay rather, even as it is, I can attack you when you are off your guard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that I shall question you, I have forewarned you; but when I shall question you I do not as yet make evident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For perhaps it may be to-day; perhaps to-morrow, perhaps after twenty or thirty days, perhaps after fewer, perhaps after more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus has God also made uncertain the day of our death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nor hath He allowed it be clear to us, whether it shall befall us to-day, or to-morrow, or after a whole year, or after many years; that through the uncertainty of the expectation we may through all time keep ourselves firm in virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And that we shall indeed depart, He hath said,—but when, He hath not yet said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus too I have said that I shall question you, but I have not added when, wishing you always to be thoughtful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And let no man say, I heard these things four or five weeks ago, or more, and I cannot retain them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For I wish the hearer so to retain them as to have his recollection perpetual and not apt to fade, nor yet that he should disown what is spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For I wish you to retain them, not, in order to tell them to me, but that ye may have profit; and this is of most serious interest to me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let no one then say this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However, I must now begin with what follows in the epistle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then is set before us to speak on to-day?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For not to angels,” he says, “did He put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is he then discoursing concerning some other world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: No, but concerning this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore he added “whereof we speak,” that he might not allow the mind to wander away in search of some other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How then does he call it “the world to come”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Exactly as he also says in another place, “Who is the figure of him that was to come,” ( Rom. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ,) when he is speaking about Adam and Christ in the Epistle to the Romans; calling Christ according to the flesh “Him that was to come” in respect of the times of Adam, (for [then] He was to come).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So now also, since he had said, “but when he bringeth in the First-Begotten into the world”: that thou mightest not suppose that he is speaking of another world, it is made certain from many considerations and from his saying “to come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the world was to come, but the Son of God always was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This world then which was about to come, He put in subjection not to Angels but to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that this is spoken with reference to the Son (he says) is evident: for surely no one would assert the other alternative, that it had reference to Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he brings forward another testimony also and says, “but one in a certain place testified, saying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore did he not mention the name of the prophet, but hid it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea, and in other testimonies also he doth this: as when he saith, “but when He bringeth in again the First-Begotten into the world, He saith, And let all the Angels of God worship Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, I will be to Him a Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And of the Angels He saith, Who maketh His angels spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundations of the earth” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 6, 5, 7, 10 ):—so also here he saith, “but one in a certain place testified, saying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this very thing (I conceive) is the act of one that conceals himself, and shows that they were well skilled in the Scriptures; his not setting down him who uttered the testimony, but introducing it as familiar and obvious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “What is man that Thou art mindful of him, or the son of man that Thou visitest him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou madest him a little lower than the angels: Thou crownedst him with glory and honor.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Now although these things were spoken of human nature generally, they would nevertheless apply more properly to Christ according to the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this, “Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet,” belongs to Him rather than to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the Son of God visited us when we were nothing: and after having assumed our [nature], and united it to Himself, He became higher than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For,” he says, “in that He hath put all things in subjection under Him, He left nothing not put under Him: but now we see not yet all things put under Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What he means is this:—since he had said, “Until I make Thine enemies Thy footstool” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 13 ),—and it was likely that they would still be grieved,—then having inserted a few things after this parenthetically, he added this testimony in confirmation of the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that they might not say, How is it that He hath put His enemies under His feet, when we have suffered so much? he sufficiently hinted at it in the former place indeed (for the word “until” showed, not what should take place immediately, but in course of time) but here he followeth it up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For do not suppose (he says) that because they have not yet been made subject, they are not to be made subject: for that they must be made subject, is evident; for, on this account was the prophecy spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For,” he says, “in that He hath put all things under Him, He left nothing not put under Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How then is it that all things have not been put under Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because they are hereafter to be put under Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If then all things must be made subject to Him, but have not yet been made subject, do not grieve, nor trouble thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If indeed when the end were come, and all things were made subject, thou wert still suffering these things, with reason wouldst thou repine: “But now we see not yet all things put under Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The King has not yet clearly conquered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then art thou troubled when suffering affliction? the preaching [of the Gospel] hath not yet prevailed over all; it is not yet time that they should be altogether made subject.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then again there is another consolation if indeed He who is hereafter to have all put in subjection under Him, hath Himself also died and submitted to sufferings innumerable. ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But,” he says, “we see Him who was made a little lower than the angels, even Jesus, for the suffering of death”—then the good things again,—“crowned with glory and honor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou, how all things apply to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the [expression], “a little,” would rather suit Him, who was only three days in Hades, but not ourselves who are for a long time in corruption.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Likewise also the [expression] “with glory and honor” will suit Him much more than us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Again, he reminds them of the Cross, thereby effecting two things; both showing His care [for them] and persuading them to bear all things nobly, looking to the Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For (he would say) if He who is worshiped of Angels, for thy sake endured to have a little less than the Angels, much more oughtest thou who art inferior to the Angels, to bear all things for His sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he shows that the Cross is “glory and honor,” as He Himself also always calls it, saying, “That the Son of Man might be glorified” ( John xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 5 ); and, “the Son of Man is glorified.” ( John xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If then He calls the [sufferings] for His servants’ sake “glory,” much more shouldest thou the [sufferings] for the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou the fruit of the Cross, how great it is? fear not the matter: for it seemeth to thee indeed to be dismal, but it brings forth good things innumerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: From these considerations he shows the benefit of trial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he says, “That He by the grace of God should taste death for every man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “That by the grace of God,” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And He indeed because of the grace of God towards us suffered these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “He who spared not His Own Son,” he says, “but delivered Him up for us all.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He did not owe us this, but has done it of grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again in the Epistle to the Romans he says, “Much more the grace of God, and the gift by grace which is by one man Jesus Christ, hath abounded unto many.” ( Rom. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “That by the grace of God He should taste death for every man,” not for the faithful only, but even for the whole world: for He indeed died for all; But what if all have not believed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He hath fulfilled His own [part].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover he said rightly “taste death for every man,” he did not say “die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as if He really was tasting it, when He had spent a little time therein, He immediately arose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By saying then “for the suffering of death,” he signified real death, and by saying “superior to angels,” he declared the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as a physician though not needing to taste the food prepared for the sick man, yet in his care for him tastes first himself, that he may persuade the sick man with confidence to venture on the food, so since all men were afraid of death, in persuading them to take courage against death, He tasted it also Himself though He needed not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For,” He says, “the prince of this world cometh and findeth nothing in Me.” ( John xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So both the words “by grace” and “should taste death for every man,” establish this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For it became Him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the Captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He speaks here of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how again he applies the [expression] “by whom” to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Which he would not have done, had it been [an expression] of inferiority, and only applicable to the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what he says is this:—He has done what is worthy of His love towards mankind, in showing His First-born to be more glorious than all, and in setting Him forth as an example to the others, like some noble wrestler that surpasses the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “The Captain of their salvation,” that is, the Cause of their salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how great is the space between?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Both He is a Son, and we are sons; but He saves, we are saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how He both brings us together and then separates us; “bringing,” he says, “many sons unto glory”: here he brings us together,—“the Captain of their salvation,” again he separates.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “To make perfect through sufferings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then sufferings are a perfecting, and a cause of salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou that to suffer affliction is not the portion of those who are utterly forsaken; if indeed it was by this that God first honored His Son, by leading Him through sufferings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And truly His taking flesh to suffer what He did suffer, is a far greater thing than making the world, and bringing it out of things that are not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This indeed also is [a token] of His loving-kindness, but the other far more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And [the Apostle] himself also pointing out this very thing, says, “That in the ages to come He might show forth the exceeding riches of His goodness, He both raised us up together, and made us sit together in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus.” ( Eph. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For it became Him for whom are all things and by whom are all things in bringing many sons to glory, to make the Captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For (he means) it became Him who taketh tender care, and brought all things into being, to give up the Son for the salvation of the rest, the One for the many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However he did not express himself thus, but, “to make perfect through sufferings,” showing the suffering for any one, not merely profits “him,” but he himself also becomes more glorious and more perfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this too he says in reference to the faithful, comforting them by the way: for Christ was glorified then when He suffered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But when I say, He was glorified, do not suppose that there was an accession of glory to Him: for that which is of nature He always had, and received nothing in addition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For,” he says, “both He that sanctifieth, and they who are sanctified, are all of one, for which cause He is not ashamed to call them brethren.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Behold again how he brings [them] together, honoring and comforting them, and making them brethren of Christ, in this respect that they are “of one.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then again guarding himself and showing that he is speaking of that which is according to the flesh, he introduces, “For He who sanctifieth,” [i.e.] Christ, “and they who are sanctified,” ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou see how great is the difference?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He sanctifies, we are sanctified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And above he said, “the Captain of their salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For there is one God, of whom are all things.” ( 1 Cor. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For which cause He is not ashamed to call them brethren.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how again he shows the superiority?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For by saying, “He is not ashamed,” he shows that the whole comes not of the nature of the thing, but of the loving affection of Him who was “not ashamed” of anything, [yea] of His great humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For though we be “of one,” yet He sanctifieth and we are sanctified: and great is the difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover “He” is of the Father, as a true Son, that is, of His substance; “we,” as created, that is, brought out of things that are not, so that the difference is great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore he says, “He is not ashamed to call them brethren” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 12 ), “saying, I will declare Thy name unto My brethren.” ( Ps. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when He clothed Himself with flesh, He clothed Himself also with the brotherhood, and at the same time came in the brotherhood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This indeed he brings forward naturally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But this ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 13 ) “I will put my trust in Him” ( 2 Sam. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 3 ), what does it mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what follows this is also [introduced] naturally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Behold, I and the children which God hath given Me.” ( Isa. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as here He shows Himself a Father, so before, a Brother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “I will declare Thy name unto My brethren,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again he indicates the superiority and the great interval [between us], by what follows ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ): “Since then the children,” he saith, “are partakers of flesh and blood” (thou seest where he saith the likeness is? in reference to the flesh), “in like manner He also Himself took part of the same.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let all the Heretics be ashamed, let those hide their faces who say that He came in appearance and not in reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he did not say, “He took part of these” only, and then say no more; although had he said thus, it would have been sufficient, but he asserted something more, adding “in like manner,” not in appearance, he means, or by an image (since in that case “in like manner” is not preserved) but in reality; showing the brotherhood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Next he sets down also the cause of the economy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “That through death,” he says, “He might destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he points out the wonder, that by what the devil prevailed, by that was he overcome, and the very thing which was his strong weapon against the world, [namely], Death, by this Christ smote him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In this he exhibits the greatness of the conqueror’s power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou see how great good death hath wrought?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And should deliver them,” he says, “who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why (he means) do ye shudder?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why do you fear him that hath been brought to nought?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He is no longer terrible, but has been trodden under foot, hath been utterly despised; he is vile and of no account. ( 2 Tim. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what is “through fear of death were all their life-time subject to bondage”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He either means this, that he who fears death is a slave, and submits to all things rather than die; or this, that all men were slaves of death and were held under his power, because he had not yet been done away; or that men lived in continual fear, ever expecting that they should die, and being afraid of death, could have no sense of pleasure, while this fear was present with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this he hinted at in saying, “All their life-time.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He here shows that the afflicted, the harassed, the persecuted, those that are deprived of country and of substance and of all other things, spend their lives more sweetly and more freely than they of old time who were in luxury, who suffered no such afflictions, who were in continual prosperity, if indeed these “all their life-time” were under this fear and were slaves; while the others have been made free and laugh at that which they shudder at.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this is now as if, when one was being led away to a captivity leading to death, and in continual expectation of it, one should feed him up with abundant dainties (something such as this was Death of old); but now, as if some one taking away that fear together with the dainties, were to promise a contest, and propose a combat that should lead no longer to death, but to a kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of which number wouldst thou have wished to be—those who are fed up in the prison-house, while every day looking for their sentence, or those who contend much and labor willingly, that they may crown themselves with the diadem of the kingdom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how he has raised up their soul, and made them elated?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He shows too, that not death alone has been put an end to, but that thereby he also who is ever showing that war without truce against us, I mean the devil, hath been brought to nought; since he that fears not death is out of reach of the devil’s tyranny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if “skin for skin, yea all things a man would give for his life” ( Job ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 4 )—when any one has determined to disregard even this, of what henceforward will he be the slave?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He fears no one, he is in terror of no one, he is higher than all, and more free than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he that disregards his own life, much more [doth he disregard] all other things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And when the devil finds a soul such as this, he can accomplish in it none of his works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what? tell me, shall he threaten the loss of property, and degradation, and banishment from one’s country?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But these are small matters to him who “counteth not even his life dear” ( Acts xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 24 ) unto him, according to the blessed Paul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that in casting out the tyranny of death, he also overthrew the strength of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he who has learnt to study innumerable [truths] concerning the resurrection, how should he fear death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How should he shudder any more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore be ye not grieved, saying, why do we suffer such and such things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For so the victory becomes more glorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And it would not have been glorious, unless by death He had destroyed death; but the most wonderful thing is that He conquered him by the very means by which he was strong, showing in every point the abundance of His means, and the excellence of His contrivances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not then prove false to the gift bestowed on us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For we,” he says, “have received not a spirit of fear, but a spirit of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 15; 2 Tim. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us stand then nobly, laughing death to scorn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But [I pause] for it comes over me to groan bitterly [at the thought of] whither Christ hath raised us up, and whither we have brought ourselves down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when I see the wailings in the public places, the groanings over those departing life, the howlings, the other unseemly behavior, believe me, I am ashamed before those heathen, and Jews, and heretics who see it, and before all who for this cause openly laugh us to scorn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For whatever I may afterwards say, I shall talk to no purpose, when philosophizing concerning the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because the heathen do not attend to what is said by me, but to what is done by you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For they will say at once, ‘when will any of these [fellows] be able to despise death, when he is not able to see another dead?’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Beautiful things were spoken by Paul, beautiful and worthy of Heaven, and of the love of God to man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what does he say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And He shall deliver them who through fear of death, were all their life-time subject to bondage.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But ye do not allow these things to be believed, fighting against them by your deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet many things exist for this very end, God building a stronghold against it, that He might destroy this same evil custom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For tell me, what mean the bright torches?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do we not send them before as athletes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what [mean] the hymns?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do we not glorify God, and give thanks that at last He has crowned the departed one, that He has freed him from his labors, that taking away uncertainty, He has him with Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Are not the Hymns for this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is not Psalmody for this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All these are the acts of those rejoicing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For,” it is said, “is any merry? let him sing psalms.” ( Jas. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But to these things the heathen give no heed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For (one will say) do not tell me of him who is philosophical when out of the affliction, for this is nothing great or surprising;—show me a man who in the very affliction itself is philosophical, and then I will believe the resurrection,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And indeed, that women engaged in the affairs of this life should act thus is no way surprising.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet indeed this even is dreadful; for from them also is the same philosophy required.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore also Paul says, “But concerning them which are asleep, I would not have you ignorant, that ye sorrow not even as the rest who have no hope.” ( 1 Thess. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He wrote not this to solitaries, nor to perpetual virgins, but to women and men in the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But however this is not so dreadful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But when any man or woman, professing to be crucified to the world, he tears his hair, and she shrieks violently—what can be more unseemly than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Believe me when I say if things were done as they ought, such persons should be excluded for a long time from the thresholds of the Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For those who are indeed worthy of being grieved for, are these who still fear and shudder at death, who have no faith in the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: ‘But I do not disbelieve the resurrection’ (one says) ‘but I long after his society.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then, tell me, when he goes from home, and that for a long absence, dost not thou do the same?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: ‘Yea, but I do weep then also’ (she says) ‘and mourn as I long after him.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But that is the conduct of those that really long after their associates, this that of her who despairs of his return.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Think, what thou singest on that occasion, “Return unto thy rest, O my soul, for the Lord hath dealt bountifully with thee.” ( Ps. cxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “I will fear no evil, for Thou art with me.” ( Ps. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “Thou art my refuge from the affliction which encompasseth me.” ( Ps. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Think what these Psalms mean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But thou dost not give heed, but art drunk from grief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Consider carefully the funeral lamentations of others that thou mayest have a remedy in thine own case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Return, O my soul, to thy rest, for the Lord hath dealt bountifully with thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Tell me, sayest thou that the Lord hath dealt bountifully with thee, and weepest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is not this mere acting, is it not hypocrisy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if indeed thou really believest the things thou sayest, thy sorrow is superfluous: but if thou art in sport and acting a part, and thinkest these things fables, why dost thou sing psalms?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou even endure the attendants?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou not drive away the singers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But this would be the act of madmen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet far more the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the present, then, I advise you: but as time goes on, I shall treat the matter more seriously: for indeed I am greatly afraid that by this practice some grievous disease may make its way into the Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The case of the wailings then we will hereafter correct.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And meanwhile I charge and testify, both to rich and poor, both to women and men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: May God indeed grant that you all depart out of life unwailed, and according to the fitting rule fathers now grown old may be attended to their graves by sons, and mothers by daughters, and grand-children, and great grand-children, in a green old age, and that untimely death may in no case occur.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: May this then be, and this I pray, and I exhort the prelates and all of you to beseech God for each other, and to make this prayer in common.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if (which God forbid, and may it never happen) any bitter death should occur, bitter, I mean, not in its nature (for henceforth there is no bitter death, for it differs not at all from sleep), but bitter in regard of your disposition, if it should happen, and any should hire these mourning women, believe me when I say (I speak not without meaning but as I have resolved, let him who will, be angry), that person we will exclude from the Church for a long time, as we do the idolater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if Paul calls “the covetous man an idolater” ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 5 ), much more him who brings in the practices of the idolaters over a believer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, tell me, for what cause dost thou invite presbyters, and the singers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is it not to afford consolation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is it not to honor the departed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then dost thou insult him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And why dost thou make him a public show?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And why dost thou make game as on a stage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We come, discoursing of the things concerning the resurrection, instructing all, even those who have not yet been smitten, by the honor shown to him, to bear it nobly if any such thing should happen and dost thou bring those who overthrow our [teachings] as much as in them lieth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What can be worse than this ridicule and mockery?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What more grievous than this inconsistency?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Be ashamed and show reverence: but if ye will not, we cannot endure the bringing in upon the Church of practices so destructive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, it is said, “them that sin rebuke before all.” ( 1 Tim. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And as to those miserable and wretched women, we through you forbid them ever to introduce themselves into the funerals of the faithful, lest we should oblige them in good earnest to wail over their own evils, and teach them not to do these things in the ills of others, but rather to weep for their own misfortunes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For an affectionate father too, when he has a disorderly son, not only advises him not to draw near to the wicked, but puts them in fear also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Behold then, I advise you, and those women through you, that you do not invite such persons, and that they do not attend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And may God grant that my words may produce some effect, and that my threat may avail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if (which God forbid) we should be disregarded, we have no choice henceforward but to put our threat into execution, chastising you by the laws of the Church, and those women as befits them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Now if any man is obstinate and contemptuous, let him hear Christ saying even now, “If any one trespass against thee, go, tell him his fault between thee and him alone”; but if he will not be persuaded, “take with thee one or two.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if even so he contradict, “tell it to the Church, but if he shall also refuse to hear the Church, let him be unto thee as a heathen man and a publican.” ( Matt. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Now if when a man trespasses against me, and will not be persuaded, [the Lord] commands me thus to turn away from him, judge ye in what light I ought to hold him who trespasses against himself, and against God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For do not you yourselves condemn us when we come down so gently upon you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If however any man disregard the bonds which we inflict, again let Christ instruct him, saying, “Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven; and whatsoever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.” ( Matt. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For though we ourselves be miserable and good for nothing and worthy to be despised, as indeed we are; yet are we not avenging ourselves nor warding off anger, but are caring for your salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Be influenced by reverence, I beseech you, and respect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if a man bear with a friend when he attacks him more vehemently than he ought, ascertaining his object, and that he does it with kind intention, and not out of insolence; much more [should he bear with] a teacher when rebuking him, and a teacher who does not himself say these things as of authority, nor as one in the position of a ruler, but in that of a kindly guardian.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For we do not say these things as wishing to exhibit our authority, (for how could we, praying that we may never come to the trial of them?) but grieving and lamenting for you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Forgive me then, and let no man disregard the bonds of the Church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is not man who binds, but Christ who has given unto us this authority, and makes men lords of this so great dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For we indeed wish to use this power for loosing; or rather, we wish to have no need even of that, for we wish that there should not be any bound among us—we are not so miserable and wretched [as that] even though some of us are extreme good-for-nothings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If however we be compelled [so to act], forgive us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is not of our own accord, nor wishing it, but rather out of sorrow for you that are bound that we put the chains around you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if any man despise these chains, the time of judgment will come, which shall teach him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what comes after I do not wish to speak of, lest I should wound your minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For in the first place indeed we do not wish to be brought into this necessity; but if we are so brought, we fulfill our own part, we cast around the chains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And if any man burst through them, I have done my part, and am henceforth free from blame, and thou wilt have to give account to Him who commanded me to bind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For neither, when a king is sitting in public, if any of the guard who stand beside him be commanded to bind one of the attendants, and to put the chains around [him], and he should not only thrust this man away, but also break the bonds in pieces, is it the guard who suffers the insult, and not much more the King who gave the order.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if He claim as His own, the things which are done to the faithful, much more will He feel as if Himself insulted when he is insulted who has been appointed to teach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But God grant that none of those who are over this Church should be driven to the necessity of [inflicting] these bonds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as it is a good thing not to sin, so is it profitable to endure reproof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then endure the rebuke, and earnestly endeavor not to sin; and if we should sin let us bear the rebuke.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as it is an excellent thing not to be wounded, but, if this should happen, to apply the remedy to the wound, so also in this case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But God forbid that any man should need such remedies as these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But we have discoursed more vehemently for the sake of greater security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is better that I should be suspected by you of being a harsh, and severe, and self-willed person, than that you should do things not approved of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But we trust in God, that this reproof will not be unserviceable to you, but that ye will be so changed, that these discourses may be devoted to encomiums on you and to praises: that we may all be counted worthy to attain to those good things, which God hath promised to them that love Him in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost be glory, might, honor, now and for ever and world without end.
